config_files.notes: Valheim stores its graphics settings in the Windows registry under HKCU\Software\IronGate\Valheim. Keys use Unity hash suffixes. Edit via regedit or PowerShell only while Valheim is fully closed.
Low-level Unity renderer configuration. Controls API selection (DX11, DX12, Vulkan). Edited by adding key=value lines. Survives game updates as it is in the game install directory.

boot_config_keys.effect: Enables Unity's multi-threaded rendering job system. Significantly improves CPU utilisation on quad-core and above hardware.
boot_config_keys.notes: This is the single most impactful tweak for Valheim. Default is 0. Setting to 1 can add 20-40% FPS on systems with 6+ cores. Add as a new line to boot.config.

boot_config_keys.effect: Enables native graphics job rendering alongside gfx-enable-gfx-jobs. Provides additional CPU thread utilisation.
boot_config_keys.notes: Apply alongside gfx-enable-gfx-jobs=1. Some older Intel CPUs (pre-8th gen) may see instability — monitor for crashes after applying.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Reduce shadow distance and shadow quality first — these are the dominant GPU costs in Valheim's outdoor environments. Vegetation draw distance is the dominant CPU cost.
manual_only_settings.reason: Valheim uses Unity's built-in settings UI. Granular controls are in-game only.

manual_only_settings.recommendation: Medium on performance hardware. Shadows over water and large open meadow biomes are particularly expensive.
50-75% on CPU-limited hardware. Valheim's procedural forests are heavily CPU-bound for vegetation draw calls.

notes: The boot.config gfx-enable-gfx-jobs=1 tweak is the community's most widely recommended Valheim optimisation and has been validated across many hardware configurations. It should be the first recommendation for any hardware tier.
Valheim uses Unity's built-in rendering pipeline (BIRP), not URP or HDRP. Some third-party Unity optimisation guides targeting URP/HDRP do not apply.

The Mistlands, Ashlands, and Deep North biomes are significantly more GPU and CPU intensive than the earlier Meadows and Black Forest biomes. Performance expectations should be communicated per-biome.
Valheim has a dedicated server component (valheim_server.exe). Server performance is a separate concern from client graphics optimisation.
